conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you properly. To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, let me start with a basic question:

**Do you have any children you are responsible for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have Alex, who is 8 years old, and lives with me here in Manchester. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester, as I mentioned.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is anyone else also responsible for Alex's care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. I'm the one responsible for their care, and they live with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, or have they been in the past 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or care, and hasn't been. They're just living at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that information.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you.

One final question:

**Does Alex have any paid work, or are they receiving any benefits in their own name (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work - they're only 8 years old! And they don't receive any benefits in their own name either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information! I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility.

Based on what you've told me, let me review your situation against the Child Benefit eligibility rules:

**For Alex (8 years old):**
- Alex is under 16 years old ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- You are responsible for Alex and they live with you ✓
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex ✓
- Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation ✓
- Alex is not in paid work or receiving benefits in their own name ✓
- You are a UK citizen ✓

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You're responsible for Alex and they live with you
- You live in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can now proceed to make your Child Benefit claim. Please note: if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is responsible for Alex's care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Alex does not have paid work or receive benefits in their own name. The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 8-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex's care, Alex lives with them full-time, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital or care, does not work, and does not receive benefits. The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is responsible for Alex's care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Alex does not have paid work or receive benefits in their own name. The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 8-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex's care, Alex lives with them full-time, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital or care, does not work, and does not receive benefits. The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.
